2605.26004.title: MAGIC: Multimodal Alignment & Grounding-aware Instruction Coreset for Vision-Language Models
2605.26004.paper_summary: Instruction tuning of large vision-language models (LVLMs) increasingly depends on massive multimodal corpora, yet these datasets contain samples with substantial redundancy, low visual dependency, and highly imbalanced coverage of multimodal reasoning behaviors. As a result, uniform subsampling or naive score-based selection often yields suboptimal training subsets. We introduce MAGIC, a training-free, forward-only coreset selection method designed to construct compact yet behaviorally faithful subsets for multimodal instruction tuning. MAGIC is built on three intrinsic signals extracted from a pretrained VLM: Multimodal Gain, which measures the likelihood improvement obtained from visual input; Bridging Relevance, which captures the sharpness of answer-token grounding over visual tokens; and Skill-Neuron Signatures, which characterize the functional computation elicited by each sample via top-activated feed-forward neurons. MAGIC combines these signals in a three-stage pipeline: filtering low-gain examples, ranking candidates by a normalized quality objective, and performing bucket-wise budget allocation over discrete neuron signatures to preserve latent multimodal skill coverage. This formulation avoids backpropagation, auxiliary selector training, and expensive clustering in continuous activation spaces, while remaining efficient and easily deployable in existing VLMs. Across LLaVA-665K and Vision-Flan datasets, and transfer settings to large target models, LLaVA-1.5-7B and -13B, MAGIC consistently improves over strong baselines under matched 20% budgets: it achieves 100.3% relative performance to full finetuning on LLaVA-665K and 101.6% relative performance on Vision-Flan-186K, while yielding a 73.7% reduction in wall-clock run time.
2605.26004.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺語言模型（LVLMs）的指令調整越來越依賴於龐大的多模態語料庫，然而這些數據集包含了大量冗餘樣本、低視覺依賴性以及多模態推理行為的高度不平衡覆蓋。因此，均勻的子抽樣或天真的基於分數的選擇通常會產生次優的訓練子集。我們引入了MAGIC，一種無需訓練、僅向前的核心集選擇方法，旨在為多模態指令調整構建緊湊但行為忠實的子集。MAGIC建立在從預訓練的VLM中提取的三個內在信號之上：多模態增益，測量從視覺輸入中獲得的可能性改善；橋接相關性，捕捉答案標記在視覺標記上的基礎上對應的清晰度；以及技能神經元簽名，表徵每個樣本通過最活躍的前饋神經元引發的功能計算。MAGIC在三個階段的流程中結合這些信號：過濾低增益示例，根據標準化質量目標對候選者進行排名，並在離散神經元簽名上進行桶狀預算分配，以保留潛在的多模態技能覆蓋。這種公式避免了反向傳播、輔助選擇器訓練和在連續激活空間中的昂貴聚類，同時在現有的VLM中保持高效且易於部署。在LLaVA-665K和Vision-Flan數據集，以及轉移到大型目標模型LLaVA-1.5-7B和-13B的設置中，MAGIC在匹配的20%預算下始終優於強基準：在LLaVA-665K上達到100.3%的相對性能，並在Vision-Flan-186K上達到101.6%的相對性能，同時實現了73.7%的牆鐘運行時間減少。
2605.26004.author: Shristi Das Biswas et.al.
